name: get-it-done
description: Autonomous 5-agent team (Planner, Analyst, Executor, Validator + post-cycle Reflector) with a batch-aware dispatcher (/continue) that autopilots from goal to COMPLETE. Three user entrypoints: /objective sets a fresh goal, /adjust refines the active goal mid-flight (soft: append constraints, preserve artifacts; hard: rewrite goal, reset planner artifacts — both preserve progress/validation log and context), /continue runs the dispatcher loop. Stopping is a planning-time decision: planner may mark milestones with PauseAfter for natural human-review checkpoints (UX feel, real-world testing, sign-off); otherwise dispatcher runs to COMPLETE / AWAITING_HUMAN. No context guard — context exhaustion is recovered via Step 2 crash recovery on next /continue. v2 architecture: dispatcher owns all shared-state writes; sub-agents return structured `---agent-return---` YAML; tasks form an explicit DAG with milestones; EXECUTING batches heterogeneous (mix per-task / milestone validators + reworks + new executors), ANALYZING batches fan out one analyst per independent research request, all bounded by N=5; planner remains a singleton. Milestone validators gate downstream milestones on integration-level criteria. Stage 5: agent_rules seeded with v2-aware initial rules (PR-001..019, AR-001..005, ER-001..011, VR-001..006, RR-001..008).
